Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JAIMIE BOMBARD, ESQ., 415.863.5718, FOR DEFENDANT THE EPSTEIN GROUP; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE EPSTEIN GROUP'S Motion To Compel Arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending completion of the arbitration. Paragraph 17 of the parties' fee agreement requires binding arbitration. Plaintiff's opposition papers do not show why the arbitration clause (1) is unenforceable or (2) does not apply to the instant action. The Court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance to January 2014. While plaintiff provides a doctor's note explaining that he has difficulty traveling, plaintiff fails to explain why he cannot appear by telephone. A petition to compel arbitration should be adjudicated in a summary manner under CCP 1290.2. The Court continued the matter from October 1, 2013, to give plaintiff time to make arrangements to appear by telephone. Plaintiff states that he has a mental disorder that makes it difficult to concentrate. Plaintiff has not provided competent medical evidence to support that assertion. The Court notes that plaintiff filed this action while representing himself on July 30, 2013. That filing, plaintiff's oppositions, and requests for default indicate that plaintiff can engage in the litigation process. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Continued To Oct-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT THE EPSTEIN GROUP'S Motion To Compel Arbitration - Judge Miller is recused. Per order of the Presiding Judge, the motion is continued to October 7, 2013. If plaintiff cannot travel to the hearing, he may make arrangements to appear by telephone by using Court call. (See San Francisco Local Rule 8.2 A. 3.) Telephone appearances must be arranged through Court Call by calling 1-888-88-COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
